title: adenocalymma album
intro: Adenocalymma album, also known as the White-flowering Adenocalymma, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as the treatment of skin conditions like eczema and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Adenocalymma album are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
